rationale: Both evaluations award the identical score of 5/10 and cite the same substantive concerns: basic German falling short of the C1 requirement, no UML/IT-architecture leadership evidence, and a lack of documented team/project leadership. The strengths are likewise identical in substance (open-source contributions including GISCollective, full-stack/cross-platform expertise, and developer education through workshops). The wording differs run-to-run, but no concern, penalty, or shift in reasoning keys off the name change from Bogdan Szabo to Mohammed Al-Said.
